title: Electrophysiological Recordings
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4fk8","text":"This protocol explains Quantitative Real‐Time Polymerase Chain Reaction (qRT‐PCR) of lines ND1014, N1, and ND27760 from Synthetic mRNAs Drive Highly Efficient iPS Cell Differentiation to Dopaminergic Neurons. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":120,"length":87}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"2ioa2","text":"Obtain approval to work with human stem cells from an appropriate Institutional Review Board. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"tb17","text":"Please refer to the Safety Data Sheets (SDS) for safety and environmental hazards. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Perform voltage-clamp recordings at 35 °C in a chamber perfused with regular artificial cerebrospinal fluid flowing at 3 ml/minute. Regular artificial cerebrospinal fluid: 124 millimolar (mM), NaCl 2.5 millimolar (mM), KCl 1.3 millimolar (mM), MgCl2 2.5 millimolar (mM), CaCl2 1 millimolar (mM), NaH2PO4 26.2 millimolar (mM), NaHCO3 20 millimolar (mM), glucose at pH 7.4 , equilibrated with 95% O 2 and 5% CO 2 , ∼310 mosm) Patch electrodes should be pulled from borosilicate glass and have resistances of 2.0–4.0 MΩ when filled with an intracellular solution. Intracellular solution: 135 millimolar (mM), KMeSO4 5 millimolar (mM), KCl 5 millimolar (mM), HEPES , 0.25 millimolar (mM), EGTA‐free acid 2 millimolar (mM), Mg‐ATP 0.5 millimolar (mM), GTP 10 millimolar (mM), phosphocreatine‐tris at pH 7.3 , ∼290 mosm
Identify neurons using a ×10 objective mounted on an upright microscope with transmitted light.

Cell somatic recordings should be made using an Axopatch 200B amplifier in combination with pClamp 10.7 software (Molecular Devices). Voltage clamp at −70 mV. Monitor R series and R input using a 2.5‐mV 100‐ms depolarizing voltage step in each recording sweep. Filter current traces at 5 kHz, digitize at 10 kHz using a Digidata 1440 interface, and store for off‐line analysis. Monitor resting membrane potential and the action potential for more than 5 minutes before drug applications.
Visualize their neuronal somata through a ×40 water immersion objective using IR differential interference contrast optics.
Add Tetraethylammonium (TEA) and ZD 7288 (Sigma‐Aldrich) the artificial cerebrospinal fluid, to block K+ or I h currents, respectively.
creator: Anita Broellochs
